pages.full_text:
outside, especially when the sun shines.
	We stayed until October 23rd. Simon + Peter enjoyed the course, Simon like Gulliver, the only adult among 85 children, singing an octave below the others. William, John, Charles & Eve visited the Simions in the Espace & bought antiques at Gloucester Docks.

May 23rd Sat
	A long drive to __Dinas__ - 12.15-5.20 V. slow on the motorways in the Midlands. In the end, we went right through the middle of Wolverhampton, & by-roads around Shrewsbury.
	We got supper ready & had just started when it rained, carried it into the tent, carried it out for pudding, when it started to rain again! But v. warm.

Sun 24th May
	Eve went up the hill for a walk & slither with Charles, rescued by Thomas.
pages.notes: Page begins mid-entry (continuation from previous spread). 'Simions' may be a surname (family name). 'May 23rd Sat' fell on a Saturday in 1992 and 1998 within the volume's 1989-2003 range; year cannot be confirmed from this page alone. 'Dinas' is underlined — likely Dinas Dinlle or another Welsh place near Llanbedr (see right page). The October 23rd date belongs to an earlier trip (music course reference).

pages.full_text:
We all sat around doing Maths, English & French homework. The 3 boys have to do 45 min. twice a day. John can almost read Red Hen & Sly Fox by himself.
	Church at 11.30. at Talybont.

From the beach near Llanbedr.
pages.notes: Top of the right page may be cut off; no handwritten page number is visible. The bulk of the page is taken up by pencil drawings of seashells (mussels, scallop, razor clam, cockle/clam shapes — approximately 9 individual shells drawn in pencil). The caption 'From the beach near Llanbedr.' is written in pencil below the drawings. The text at the top is in blue ink and continues the Sunday 24th May entry from the left page. Talybont is a village in Ceredigion/Gwynedd, Wales; Llanbedr is in Gwynedd near Barmouth.
